title: Report placement: HTML in plugin state dir, PDF in prompt folder
cause: After report-gen.py produces HTML and html-to-pdf.py converts it, the HTML lingered in the prompt folder. User flagged clutter — prompt folder should carry only final deliverables.
counter: After generating a Flux PDF, sweep the prompt folder for *.html and move or delete. Only prompt.<ext>, metadata.json, tests.json, learnings.{md,json}, output-schema.json, and report.pdf belong in prompts/<name>/. Intermediate files go in plugins/prompt-crafter/state/.
signal: If you see a .html or ._tmp_* file next to prompt.xml, you skipped cleanup. Sweep before declaring done.

title: Every new Flux prompt folder ships the full canonical file set pre-run
cause: Delivered partial prompt folders (prompt.xml + metadata + tests + learnings.md only) multiple times. User had to call it out repeatedly. Reference prompts (reaper/, reaper-patterns/, redteam-ops-architecture/) carry a richer set and the gap was visible.
counter: Pre-run minimum on every new prompt folder: prompt.<ext>, metadata.json, tests.json, learnings.md, learnings.json (sessions/hypotheses/axis-progressions shape), output-schema.json. Post-run additions: output-reference.md, output-test-results.json, report.pdf. Diff against reaper/, reaper-patterns/, redteam-ops-architecture/ before declaring ready.
signal: Before handing a prompt over, enumerate files in the folder. If count < 6 pre-run or < 8 post-run, the set is incomplete.

title: .gitkeep only in empty dirs — never alongside real content
cause: Scaffolded directories with .gitkeep, then added real content without removing the .gitkeep. Visible sloppiness signal to reviewers across enchanted-plugins siblings.
counter: A directory has real tracked content OR a .gitkeep — never both. When adding the first real file, remove the .gitkeep in the same commit. Sweep before committing plugin scaffolds: for d in $(find . -type d); do [[ -f $d/.gitkeep ]] && [[ $(ls -A $d | grep -v gitkeep | wc -l) -gt 0 ]] && echo redundant: $d/.gitkeep; done
signal: Cross-repo rule: applies to flux, allay, hornet, reaper, weaver, mantis, nook, schematic, and every future sibling.

title: Read flux/docs/* directly for ecosystem facts, not auto-memory
cause: Cited a 3-day-old auto-memory snapshot of the plugin roadmap instead of reading flux/docs/ROADMAP.md. Memory was already stale — Mantis had shipped but the memory still listed Phase 3 Athena.
counter: For ecosystem facts (plugin list, roadmap, naming, status) always read the live docs first: flux/docs/ROADMAP.md, flux/docs/ecosystem.md, flux/docs/brand-guide.md, and the filesystem under c:/git/enchanted-skills/. Auto-memory is for user preferences, not ecosystem state.
signal: When the user asks 'what plugins exist?' or 'what's next?' or 'what's the naming rule?', the first tool call should be Read on a docs file or Bash ls on the repo, not memory recall.

title: Ran seven reactive iterations instead of one-pass Flux lifecycle
cause: Generated a Flux prompt artifact (ten-golden-circle-research) by drafting v1 and handing over, then reacting to each user pushback as an isolated event: add living-repo audit, re-converge, add Casting Doubt, flatten metadata shape, add tests.json, fix format key, hoist sigma. Each fix was a schema/lifecycle-stage issue that /test-prompt or /converge would have surfaced pre-ship.
counter: Run the full Flux lifecycle in one pass before handing over: /create → /converge (Sonnet optimizer + Haiku reviewer, not heuristic loops) → /test-prompt (surfaces tests.json + metadata shape + format/registry mismatch) → /harden (red-team audit on high-stakes prompts) → hand over with honest verdict. Heuristic self-eval is a sanity check, not convergence.
signal: When generating any prompt artifact for Flux, walk the four lifecycle stages in order before the user sees the result. If you find yourself applying a schema fix because the user flagged a warning, the lifecycle was skipped.

title: R8 labeled 'Bayesian Threat Convergence' but implementation is pure EMA
cause: Reaper's R8 engine was documented across 15 touch points (README, CLAUDE.md, audit-trail/README, constants.sh, science/README, learnings.py, mermaid diagrams, ROADMAP) as 'Bayesian Threat Convergence.' The actual code in shared/scripts/learnings.py is a single-line EMA update (r_new = alpha*s_current + (1-alpha)*r_prior, alpha=0.3) with zero Bayesian priors, posteriors, or inference. Beta(2,8) priors declared in constants.sh as REAPER_THREAT_PRIOR_ALPHA/BETA are never read by any code path. The 'Bayesian' label was aspirational, never implemented. Caught by the learning-ecosystem-research panel's Casting Doubt on 'Gauss Accumulation' — only Mantis M6 is genuinely Bayesian across the ecosystem; Reaper R8 and five other per-plugin learning engines are EMA-class.
counter: Honest naming contract: every algorithm name must match what the code does, not what the roadmap aspires to. When declaring an engine 'Bayesian / Gauss / Thompson / Beta-Binomial,' verify prior and posterior update steps exist in code. If only a decay rule exists, call it EMA / exponential smoothing / posture decay. Taxonomy across enchanted-plugins: EMA-class (Reaper R8, Nook L1, Allay A2), Beta-Binomial-class (Mantis M6), Prose-log-class (Flux F6 learnings.json, precedent.jsonl).
signal: Before shipping any engine with 'Bayesian' in its name, grep the implementation for actual prior/posterior usage. If the only parameter is a learning rate alpha, the honest name is EMA. Apply to future ecosystem engines the same test the research caught here.
